ocr.text: In recent years, the automotive industry has witnessed a significant shift towards the integration of autonomous driving
technologies, prompting companies to allocate substantial resources for research and development (R&D) in this area. As a
leader in the automotive and mobility sector, BYD Company has recognized the importance of advancing its capabilities in
autonomous driving through increased investment. The data table presented herein outlines BYD's autonomous driving R&D
spend across several key metrics, including annual expenditures for the years 2021 and 2022, as well as specific allocations for
software integration and overall project funding. Notably, BYD's R&D expenditure in 2021 was recorded at USD 700 million,
reflecting a robust commitment to enhancing its technological prowess. In 2022, this investment escalated to USD 800 million,

underscoring the company's strategic focus on innovation and competitive advantage in a rapidly evolving market.

Moreover,
the table delineates the company's expenditures on software integration, with a significant emphasis placed on
VehicleAutomation solutions. This metric is critical as software plays a pivotal role in the successful deployment of autonomous
driving systems, necessitating advanced algorithms, machine learning capabilities, and real-time data processing. In line with
this focus, BYD's allocation for software integration in 2022 was reported as USD 260 million. This funding is indicative of the
company's recognition that seamless integration of software solutions is essential for the development of reliable and safe

autonomous vehicles.

Furthermore, the report highlights the overall trends in autonomous driving R&D spending within the
industry, illustrating a growing commitment from automotive manufacturers to push the boundaries of technology and
innovation. For instance, BYD's R&D spend in the category of "22" reflected a notable increase with a value of USD 19
million, emphasizing the company's targeted initiatives aimed at specific aspects of autonomous technology development. This
strategic investment is part of a broader trend where manufacturers are increasingly prioritizing R&D in order to stay

competitive in a market that is becoming increasingly reliant on cutting-edge technology.

As the automotive landscape
continues to evolve, BYD's strategic R&D investments are poised to position the company favorably in the competitive arena of
autonomous driving.
Year  Amount($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  R&DFocus  TotalUnits  GrowthRate(%)
2019  150  10  5  SensorTechnology  500  15
2020  180  20  6  AIDevelopment  600  12
2021  220  22  7  SoftwareIntegration  700  10
2022  [HIDDEN]  18  8  DataAnalytics  [HIDDEN]  15
2023  310  [HIDDEN]  9  [HIDDEN]  900  [HIDDEN]
The increase from USD 700 million in 2021 to USD 800 million in 2022 illustrates a clear upward trajectory in spending, which
is essential for enhancing the company's capabilities in vehicle automation. Additionally, with an allocation of USD 18 million
designated for various ancillary projects, BYD is exploring diverse avenues for innovation beyond its core R&D initiatives.

This multifaceted approach ensures that the company remains agile and responsive to the demands of a dynamic market.

In
conclusion, BYD's commitment to autonomous driving R&D, as reflected in the data presented, highlights the company's
proactive stance in embracing technological advancements. The strategic investments in software integration and overall R&D
expenditures showcase BYD's determination to lead in the autonomous vehicle sector. As competition intensifies, it is
imperative for companies in the automotive industry to continually adapt and innovate, and BYD's increasing R&D spend
positions it as a formidable player in the future of mobility.
